title: Introduction
description: Run your first Python code and understand how ML code starts.
takeaways: Machine learning learns patterns from data instead of hard-coded rules.
Supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning are the main types.
Python notebooks are a practical way to start experimenting with ML code.

video.title: How to create a new project
video.description: This video shows you how to create a new project in the project management tool.

practiceLab.objective: Run your first notebook cells and confirm that Python lists and NumPy arrays print correctly in the Practice Lab.
instructions: Read the markdown cell titled Machine Learning Introduction.
Run the first code cell and confirm that Hello WebAIGenAcademy! prints.
Optional: edit the comment that says add your name her and replace it with your name as a print statement, then re-run the cell.
Run the second code cell to create the numbers list [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] and print it.
Run the third code cell to import NumPy, create the array [10, 20, 30], and print the result.
If any cell fails, restart the kernel and re-run the cells from top to bottom.
hints: Use Shift+Enter (or the Run button) to execute a highlighted cell.
NumPy must import successfully before data = np.array(...) can run.
Keep cell order: list practice, then NumPy practice.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Console output showing Hello WebAIGenAcademy!, the list [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], and the NumPy array [10 20 30].
